function.description: Defines a reusable function. Supports parameters, pipeline input, and output.

param.description: Parameter block for functions and scripts. Supports mandatory, default values, validation.

if.description: Conditional execution. Conditions use comparison operators like -eq, -ne, -gt, -lt, -like, -match.

foreach.description: Iterate over each item in a collection.

try_catch.description: Error handling. $_ in catch block contains the error. finally always executes.

pipeline.description: Pipeline passes objects (not text) between commands. Each command processes the output of the previous.

Get-Content.description: Reads content from a file. Alias: gc, cat, type.

Set-Content.description: Writes content to a file. Replaces existing content. Alias: sc.

Get-ChildItem.description: Lists files and directories. Alias: gci, ls, dir.

Where-Object.description: Filters objects in the pipeline. Alias: where, ?.

Select-Object.description: Selects specific properties or a subset of objects. Alias: select.

ForEach-Object.description: Processes each pipeline object. $_ is the current object. Alias: foreach, %.

Invoke-WebRequest.description: Makes HTTP/HTTPS requests. Alias: iwr, curl, wget.

Get-Service.description: Gets Windows services. Can filter by name or status.

Get-Process.description: Gets running processes. Alias: gps, ps.

comparison_operators.description: PowerShell comparison operators. -like uses wildcards, -match uses regex.

hashtable.description: Hash table (dictionary) literal. Keys are strings, values can be any type.

array.description: Array literal. @() creates an empty array. Arrays are zero-indexed.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.
